title: register_follow_ups lifecycle phase on the Tool trait
purpose: Extend the tool lifecycle with a register_follow_ups phase that runs after on-success (and after error for failure-recovery follow-ups). This is the mechanism by which tools declare what follow-up actions are available on their result. The phase is optional — tools that do not override it return an empty Vec. The registry attaches returned follow-ups to the tool result under a follow_ups key.
task: Add a register_follow_ups method to the Tool trait in traits.rs with a default implementation returning empty Vec<FollowUpAction>. Modify the lifecycle dispatch in registry.rs to call register_follow_ups after on_success (on success path) and after execute error handling (on error path for failure-recovery follow-ups). The registry SHALL attach the returned Vec<FollowUpAction> to the tool output by injecting a follow_ups array (using model_facing_json) into the output content. When the follow-ups Vec is empty, no follow_ups key is added.

requirements.title: Add register_follow_ups to the Tool trait
requirements.spec: The Tool trait in crates/norn/src/tool/traits.rs SHALL gain a new async method: async fn register_follow_ups(&self, output: &ToolOutput, ctx: &ToolContext) -> Vec<FollowUpAction>. The default implementation SHALL return Vec::new(). The method receives the tool output and context so it can inspect the result to decide which follow-ups to offer.
acceptance: Tool trait in traits.rs declares register_follow_ups with the specified signature
Default implementation returns Vec::new()
Existing tools that do not override register_follow_ups continue to compile without changes
The method is async and takes &self, &ToolOutput, &ToolContext
cargo check -p norn succeeds

requirements.title: Call register_follow_ups on the success path in registry dispatch
requirements.spec: The ToolExecutor impl in registry.rs SHALL call tool.register_follow_ups(&output, &ctx) after the on_success phase completes. The returned Vec<FollowUpAction> SHALL be attached to the tool output: if non-empty, inject a follow_ups array into output.content (which is a serde_json::Value) containing the model_facing_json() of each FollowUpAction. If the Vec is empty, output.content is unchanged.
acceptance: register_follow_ups is called after on_success in the lifecycle dispatch
A tool returning 2 FollowUpActions has output.content["follow_ups"] with 2 entries
Each entry in follow_ups has exactly action, description, expires keys (from model_facing_json)
A tool returning empty Vec has no follow_ups key in output.content
The full Vec<FollowUpAction> is retained internally for action log storage (not discarded after serialization)

requirements.title: Call register_follow_ups on the error path for failure-recovery follow-ups
requirements.spec: When a tool's execute() returns an error, or when post-validate fails, the registry SHALL still call register_follow_ups if the error is a domain error (not a system/infrastructure error). Specifically: after PostValidateOutcome::Fail in Gate mode, before returning ToolError::PostValidationFailed, the registry SHALL call register_follow_ups with the output that was produced (the output exists — it was committed before post-validate ran). The returned follow-ups SHALL be stored for action log indexing. For execute-phase errors (ToolError), register_follow_ups is NOT called — there is no output to inspect.
acceptance: Gate-mode post-validate failure still calls register_follow_ups with the committed output
Follow-ups from failure path are stored for action log indexing (retained on the internal result, not discarded)
Execute-phase errors do NOT trigger register_follow_ups
Pre-validate blocks do NOT trigger register_follow_ups

requirements.title: Expose follow-ups alongside dispatch result for action log consumption
requirements.spec: The ToolExecutor dispatch method SHALL return the Vec<FollowUpAction> alongside the ToolOutput. The return type changes from Result<ToolOutput, ToolError> to Result<(ToolOutput, Vec<FollowUpAction>), ToolError>. Callers that do not need follow-ups can destructure and discard. For PostValidationFailed errors, the follow-ups SHALL be accessible via a new committed_follow_ups field on ToolError::PostValidationFailed (or a separate side-channel). The action log (future brief NTA-001) indexes these.
acceptance: ToolExecutor::execute returns Result<(ToolOutput, Vec<FollowUpAction>), ToolError>
All callers of ToolExecutor::execute updated to handle the new return type
Follow-ups from the failure path are not silently dropped
cargo check -p norn succeeds with all callers updated

requirements.title: Tests for lifecycle extension
requirements.spec: Tests SHALL verify: (1) a tool with no register_follow_ups override produces empty follow-ups and no follow_ups key in output, (2) a tool that returns follow-ups has them injected into output.content["follow_ups"], (3) Gate-mode post-validate failure still produces follow-ups, (4) execute-phase error does not call register_follow_ups. Tests SHALL use the existing test stub pattern from registry.rs tests.
acceptance: Test: default register_follow_ups returns empty Vec and output has no follow_ups key
Test: tool returning 2 follow-ups has output.content["follow_ups"].as_array().len() == 2
Test: Gate post-validate failure returns follow-ups alongside the error
Test: execute error does not trigger register_follow_ups
All tests pass with cargo test -p norn

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement any tool's register_follow_ups override — individual tool follow-up registration is NTF-004
SHALL NOT implement the follow_up tool — that is NTF-003
SHALL NOT implement action log storage of follow-ups — that is NTA-001/NTA-004
SHALL NOT implement expiry checking — that is NTF-003
SHALL NOT modify ToolError::PostValidationFailed to add committed_output — that is a shared prerequisite (PR0) handled by the conventions cluster
verification: cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo fmt -p norn -- --check produces no diff
cargo test -p norn passes all tests (existing and new)
grep -rn 'todo!\|unimplemented!\|FIXME\|HACK\|TODO' crates/norn/src/tool/traits.rs crates/norn/src/tool/registry.rs returns no matches
Existing tools compile without modifications (default register_follow_ups returns empty Vec)
